dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7824 IH: Federal Funds Whistleblower Protection Extension Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Fischbach (for herself and Mr. Finstad) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

form.official-title: To extend whistleblower protections to individuals who disclose misuse of Federal funds administered by State and local officials, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Federal Funds Whistleblower Protection Extension Act.

section.header: Extension of whistleblower protections to State-administered Federal funds
section.text: Subpart F of part III of title 5, United States Code, is amended by inserting after chapter 75 the following new chapter:

chapter.header: Protection of Whistleblowers in Federally Funded State Programs

paragraph.text: The term covered individual means any employee, contractor, subgrantee, or agent of a State or local government, or of a non-Federal entity, administering or receiving Federal financial assistance.

The term covered official means any officer, employee, or agent of a State or local government acting in connection with the administration, distribution, or oversight of Federal financial assistance.
The term protected disclosure means any lawful disclosure of information that the covered individual reasonably believes evidences—

subparagraph.text: misuse, waste, fraud, or abuse of Federal funds;
a violation of Federal law relating to a federally funded program; or
gross mismanagement of a program receiving Federal financial assistance.

subsection.text: A covered official may not take or fail to take a personnel action against a covered individual because of a protected disclosure.
For purposes of this section, personnel action includes—

paragraph.text: significant change in duties or responsibilities;

subsection.text: Any covered official who knowingly retaliates against a covered individual for making a protected disclosure under this chapter shall be subject to criminal penalties under this section.
Penalties shall be imposed according to the level of culpability:

paragraph.text: A covered official who engages in retaliation due to negligence shall be fined not more than $50,000.
A covered official who knowingly engages in retaliation shall be fined not more than $100,000, imprisoned not more than 1 year, or both.

paragraph.header: Intentional retaliation for personal gain or concealment
paragraph.text: A covered official who retaliates with the intent to conceal misuse of Federal funds or to obtain personal benefit shall be fined not more than $250,000, imprisoned not more than 5 years, or both.

section.text: If a Federal agency or Inspector General determines that there is reasonable cause to believe that retaliation prohibited under this chapter has occurred, the agency may refer the matter to the Attorney General for investigation and criminal prosecution.

subsection.text: As a condition of receiving Federal financial assistance, each State or local government shall certify compliance with this chapter.
Failure to comply may result in—

paragraph.text: termination of Federal funding for the affected program.

section.text: The table of chapters for part III of title 5, United States Code, is amended by inserting after the item relating to chapter 75 the following new item:

multi-column-toc-entry.toc-enum: 76. Protection of Whistleblowers in Federally Funded State Programs
